questionText: Quantify the amount and percentage share of your spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition.
questionKo: 귀사의 전체 비용 / 매출액 가운데 기후전환계획에 부합하는 비용 / 매출액의 금액과 비율을 입력하십시오 .
sectorTags: Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: This question allows organizations to demonstrate the extent to which their spending and revenue is compatible with their climate transition, by assessing their alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy and/or any other methodology. Such reporting is aimed at eliminating greenwashing in the organization's financial planning in relation to its climate transition.

columns.text: Methodology or framework used to assess alignment
columns.textKo: 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment

columns.text: Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy-eligible in the reporting year (%)

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes" in response to column "Identification of spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition" of 5.4.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "예" in response to 열 "Identification of spending/매출 that is aligned with 귀사의 climate transition" of 5.4.

guidance.text:
General 
This question aims to understand your organization's unique financial pathway associated with its climate transition.
It is up to each organization to disclose the relevant financial metrics and methodologies for identifying the alignment of its expenditures/revenues with its climate transition. 
Note that this question requests information to be provided at the organization level. If you indicated in column 3 "Indicate the level at which…" of 5.4 that you identify alignment at both the organization and activity level, you will have the opportunity to provide activity-level information in the subsequent question, 5.4.2.
If you are reporting any type of spending on or revenue from low-carbon products and/or services, specify in column 12 "Details of the methodology…" whether it pertains to mature technologies or non-mature technologies (e.g., if you finance Emerging Climate Technologies). If this pertains to both mature and non-mature technologies, provide the breakdown for these. 
It is acknowledged that figures for future years will be estimates. Assumptions underlying these estimates should be disclosed in column 12 "Details of the methodology…". 
Methodology or framework used to assess alignment (column 1) 
The options presented in this column depend on your selection in column 2 "Methodology or framework used…" of 5.4. 
Select the methodology against which you would like to report spending and revenue that is aligned with your climate transition. 
If the sustainable finance taxonomy used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition does not cover certain activities, projects, or assets in your operations, add rows to disclose the alignment of your financial metrics according to other methodologies used by your organization to complement your taxonomy reporting. For instance, if you are disclosing against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, you may add rows to report alignment against a bespoke methodology for assessing the alignment of activities not eligible under the taxonomy (e.g. wholesale and retail trade, manufacturing of food, beverages, and tobacco etc.). 
Provide details of the extent and basis of application of different methodologies to assess the alignment of your spending/revenue with your organization's climate transition in column 12 "Details of the methodology…".
Taxonomy under which information is being reported (column 2) 
This column is only presented if "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is selected in column 1 "Methodology or framework used to assess alignment". 
Add a row for each sustainable finance taxonomy you are providing information for.
Select "Other, please specify" to provide information for a sustainable finance taxonomy that is not listed. For further information on alternative sustainable finance taxonomies, please see CDP's policy brief .
If you select "EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities", note that the reporting period under the EU Taxonomy is January to December which may differ from the reporting period indicated in 1.4 relevant to the rest of the CDP questionnaire.
Objective under which alignment is being reported (column 3) 
This column is only presented if "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is selected in column 1 "Methodology or framework used to assess alignment". 
Add a row for each climate-related objective within the sustainable finance taxonomy you wish to provide information for. See the Explanation of Terms for more information on climate change mitigation and adaptation. If the sustainable finance taxonomy does not require disclosure against specific climate-related objectives, select the objective that best matches the criteria specified by the taxonomy or report the information as a total by selecting "Total across climate change mitigation and climate change adaptation".
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should enter information for each climate-related objective separately. You should also report total alignment across both climate-related objectives. The figures reported in this question should be calculated from the "Taxonomy-aligned" information you report at activity-level in the subsequent question 5.4.2.
Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information for the selected objective (column 4) 
This column only appears if "EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities" is selected in column 2 "Taxonomy under which information is being reported".
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should select "Yes" if they wish to report the share of their financial metrics that are eligible and non-eligible per objective under the EU Taxonomy.
Financial metric (column 5) 
Add a row for each financial metric you would like to provide information for, or select "Other, please specify" to provide information for a financial metric that is not listed. 
You can make your response more granular by adding multiple rows and selecting "Other, please specify". For example, if in addition to total OPEX, you wish to report several distinct categories of OPEX (e.g., utilities, business travel, R&D expenses, etc.) separately, you may do so by adding multiple rows and using "Other, please specify" to specify the relevant OPEX category. 
Note that the "Other, please specify" option only appears if you select "Other, please specify" in either column 1 "Methodology or framework used to assess alignment" or column 2 "Taxonomy under which information is being reported".
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should add a separate row to provide figures for turnover, CAPEX and if relevant, OPEX associated with each environmental objective separately, and a total across all objectives. Note that as per the EU Taxonomy Technical Expert Group Report (TEG), "turnover" and "revenue" are often used interchangeably, and in some contexts, may mean the same thing, despite there being some technical differences. The term turnover is most commonly used in Europe and Asia, while the use of the terms revenues or sales is more common in the United States. Revenue disclosures can therefore be considered as turnover wherever appropriate.
Amount of selected financial metric that is aligned in the reporting year (column 6) 
Enter the spending/revenue that you consider to be aligned with your organization's climate transition as per your chosen methodology/framework as indicated in column 1 "Methodology or framework...") for this financial metric as an absolute monetary value in the reporting year. 
This figure should be based on your organization-wide financial statement for the reporting year, consistent with your reporting boundary as disclosed in 1.5, and in the same currency that you selected in question 1.2 for all financial information disclosed throughout your response. 
Unless your organization is disclosing alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy which requires data to be provided for a specific reporting year, the figure provided in this column should be consistent with the reporting year defined by your answer to 1.4. 
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should provide this figure for a January to December reporting period. If you are disclosing information under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, the figure reported in this column should meet the requirements of Article 3 of the EU Taxonomy Regulation .
Percentage share of selected financial metric aligned in the reporting year (%) (column 7) 
Enter the spending/revenue that you consider to be aligned with your organization's climate transition as per your chosen methodology/ framework as indicated in column 1 "Methodology or framework...") for this financial metric as a percentage of your total spending/revenue for this financial metric in the reporting year. 
This figure should be based on your organization-wide financial statement for the reporting year, consistent with your reporting boundary as disclosed in 1.5. 
Unless your organization is disclosing alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy which requires data to be provided for a specific reporting year, the figure provided in this column should be consistent with the reporting year defined by your answer to 1.4. 
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should provide this figure for a January to December reporting period. If you are disclosing information under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, the figure reported in this column should meet the requirements of Article 3 of the EU Taxonomy Regulation .
Percentage share of selected financial metric planned to align in 2030 (%) (column 8) 
Enter the spending/revenue for this financial metric that you plan to align with your organization's climate transition as per your chosen methodology/framework as indicated in column 1 "Methodology or framework...") a percentage of your total planned spending/revenue for this financial metric in 2030.
Percentage share of selected financial metric planned to align in 2035 (%) (column 9) 
Enter the spending/revenue for this financial metric that you plan to align with your organization's climate transition (i.e., as per your chosen methodology/framework as indicated in column 1 "Methodology or framework...") as a percentage of your total planned spending/revenue for this financial metric in 2035.
Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy-eligible in the reporting year (column 10) 
This column appears only if "Yes" is selected in column 3 "Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information for the selected objective". 
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should use this column to enter their spending/revenue that is assessed to be eligible under the EU Taxonomy. This figure should include the spending/revenue that is assessed to be aligned.
Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy non-eligible in the reporting year (column 11) 
This column appears only if "Yes" is selected in column 3 "Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information for the selected objective". 
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should use this column to enter their spending/revenue that is assessed to be non-eligible under the EU Taxonomy. 
Details of the methodology or framework used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition (column 12) 
Provide the criteria used to determine the alignment of the spending/revenue with your organization's climate transition, including:
Examples of the activities, assets, technologies, products and/or services for which you classified the associated spending/revenue as aligned with your organization's climate transition, as per your chosen methodology.
Examples of activities, assets, technologies, products and/or services for which you did not classify the associated spending/revenue as aligned.
Comment on how your organization's spending/revenue that is aligned with your climate transition is estimated to change over time and describe the assumptions underlying the estimation.
Organizations that selected "Other, please specify" in column 1 "Methodology or framework used to assess alignment" should indicate whether you have obtained third party verification/assurance for your alignment information.
Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should use this column to describe your methodology for calculating your taxonomy alignment and eligibility and how you have avoided double-counting. If you have obtained third party verification/assurance for your alignment information, you will have the opportunity to indicate this in a subsequent question.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 aims to understand 귀사의 unique financial pathway associated with its climate transition. It is up to each organization to disclose the relevant financial metrics 및 methodologies for identifying the alignment of its expenditures/매출s with its climate transition. Note that this 문항 requests information to be 입력 at the organization level. 만약 you indicated in 열 3 "Indicate the level at which…" of 5.4 that you identify alignment at both the organization 및 activity level, you will have the opportunity to provide activity-level information in the subsequent 문항, 5.4.2. 만약 you are reporting 하나 이상 type of spending on 또는 매출 from low-carbon products 및/또는 services, specify in 열 12 "Details 의 방법론…" whether it pertains to mature technologies 또는 non-mature technologies (e.g., 만약 you finance Emerging Climate Technologies). 만약 this pertains to both mature 및 non-mature technologies, provide the breakdown 에 대한se. It is acknowledged that figures for future years will be estimates. Assumptions underlying these estimates 해야 합니다 be 공개 in 열 12 "Details 의 방법론…". 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment (열 1) The options presented in this 열 depend on your selection in 열 2 "방법론 또는 framework used…" of 5.4. Select the 방법론 against which you would like to report spending 및 매출 that is aligned with your climate transition. 만약 the sustainable finance taxonomy used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition does not cover certain activities, projects, 또는 assets in your operations, add 행 to disclose the alignment of your financial metrics according to 기타 방법론 used by 귀사 to complement your taxonomy reporting. For instance, 만약 you are disclosing against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, you may add 행 to report alignment against a bespoke 방법론 for assessing the alignment of activities not 획득 가능 under the taxonomy (e.g. wholesale 및 retail trade, manufacturing of food, beverages, 및 tobacco etc.). 세부 정보를 입력 의 extent 및 basis of application of different methodologies to assess the alignment of your spending/매출 with 귀사의 climate transition in 열 12 "Details 의 방법론…". Taxonomy under which information is being 보고 (열 2) This 열 is only presented 만약 "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is 선택 in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment". Add a 행 for each sustainable finance taxonomy you are providing information for. Select "Other, 직접 입력" to provide information for a sustainable finance taxonomy that is not listed. For further information on alternative sustainable finance taxonomies, please see CDP's 정책 brief . 만약 you select "EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities", note that the reporting period under the EU Taxonomy is January to December which may differ from the reporting period indicated in 1.4 relevant to the rest 의 CDP questionnaire. Objective under which alignment is being 보고 (열 3) This 열 is only presented 만약 "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is 선택 in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment". Add a 행 for each climate-related objective within the sustainable finance taxonomy you wish to provide information for. See the Explanation of Terms for more information on climate change mitigation 및 adaptation. 만약 the sustainable finance taxonomy does not require disclosure against specific climate-related objectives, select the objective that best matches the criteria specified by the taxonomy 또는 report the information as a total by selecting "Total across climate change mitigation 및 climate change adaptation". Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 enter information for each climate-related objective separately. You 해야 합니다 also report total alignment across both climate-related objectives. The figures 보고 in this 문항 해야 합니다 be calculated from the "Taxonomy-aligned" information you report at activity-level in the subsequent 문항 5.4.2. Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information 에 대한 선택 objective (열 4) This 열 only appears 만약 "EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities" is 선택 in 열 2 "Taxonomy under which information is being 보고". Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 select "예" 만약 they wish to report the share 의ir financial metrics that are 획득 가능 및 non-획득 가능 per objective under the EU Taxonomy. Financial metric (열 5) Add a 행 for each financial metric you would like to provide information for, 또는 select "Other, 직접 입력" to provide information for a financial metric that is not listed. You can make your response more granular by adding multiple 행 및 selecting "Other, 직접 입력". For example, 만약 in addition to total 운영비용(운영비용(OPEX)), you wish to report several distinct categories of 운영비용(운영비용(OPEX)) (e.g., utilities, business travel, R&D expenses, etc.) separately, you may do so by adding multiple 행 및 using "Other, 직접 입력" to specify the relevant 운영비용(운영비용(OPEX)) category. Note that the "Other, 직접 입력" option only appears 만약 you select "Other, 직접 입력" in either 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment" 또는 열 2 "Taxonomy under which information is being 보고". Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 add a separate 행 to provide figures for turnover, 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) 및 만약 relevant, 운영비용(운영비용(OPEX)) associated with each environmental objective separately, 및 a total across all objectives. Note that as per the EU Taxonomy Technical Expert Group Report (TEG), "turnover" 및 "매출" are often used interchangeably, 및 in some contexts, may mean the same thing, despite there being some technical differences. The term turnover is most commonly used in Europe 및 Asia, while the use 의 terms 매출s 또는 sales is more common in the United States. 매출 disclosures can therefore be considered as turnover wherever appropriate. Amount of 선택 financial metric that is aligned in the 보고연도 (열 6) Enter the spending/매출 that you consider to be aligned with 귀사의 climate transition as per your chosen 방법론/framework as indicated in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework...") for this financial metric as an absolute monetary value in the 보고연도. This figure 해야 합니다 be based on 귀사-wide financial statement 에 대한 보고연도, consistent with your 보고경계 as 공개 in 1.5, 및 in the same currency that you 선택 in 문항 1.2 for all financial information 공개 throughout your response. Unless 귀사 is disclosing alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy which requires data to be 입력 for a specific 보고연도, the figure 입력 in this 열 해야 합니다 be consistent with the 보고연도 defined by your answer to 1.4. Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 provide this figure for a January to December reporting period. 만약 you are disclosing information under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, the figure 보고 in this 열 해야 합니다 meet the requirements of Article 3 의 EU Taxonomy Regulation . Percentage share of 선택 financial metric aligned in the 보고연도 (%) (열 7) Enter the spending/매출 that you consider to be aligned with 귀사의 climate transition as per your chosen 방법론/ framework as indicated in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework...") for this financial metric as a percentage of your total spending/매출 for this financial metric in the 보고연도. This figure 해야 합니다 be based on 귀사-wide financial statement 에 대한 보고연도, consistent with your 보고경계 as 공개 in 1.5. Unless 귀사 is disclosing alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy which requires data to be 입력 for a specific 보고연도, the figure 입력 in this 열 해야 합니다 be consistent with the 보고연도 defined by your answer to 1.4. Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 provide this figure for a January to December reporting period. 만약 you are disclosing information under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, the figure 보고 in this 열 해야 합니다 meet the requirements of Article 3 의 EU Taxonomy Regulation . Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2030 (%) (열 8) Enter the spending/매출 for this financial metric that you plan to align with 귀사의 climate transition as per your chosen 방법론/framework as indicated in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework...") a percentage of your total planned spending/매출 for this financial metric in 2030. Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2035 (%) (열 9) Enter the spending/매출 for this financial metric that you plan to align with 귀사의 climate transition (i.e., as per your chosen 방법론/framework as indicated in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework...") as a percentage of your total planned spending/매출 for this financial metric in 2035. Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 (열 10) This 열 appears only 만약 "예" is 선택 in 열 3 "Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information 에 대한 선택 objective". Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 use this 열 to enter their spending/매출 that is assessed to be 획득 가능 under the EU Taxonomy. This figure 해야 합니다 include the spending/매출 that is assessed to be aligned. Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy non-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 (열 11) This 열 appears only 만약 "예" is 선택 in 열 3 "Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information 에 대한 선택 objective". Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 use this 열 to enter their spending/매출 that is assessed to be non-획득 가능 under the EU Taxonomy. Details 의 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition (열 12) Provide the criteria used to determine the alignment 의 spending/매출 with 귀사의 climate transition, 포함: Examples 의 activities, assets, technologies, products 및/또는 services for which you classified the associated spending/매출 as aligned with 귀사의 climate transition, as per your chosen 방법론. Examples of activities, assets, technologies, products 및/또는 services for which you did not classify the associated spending/매출 as aligned. Comment on how 귀사의 spending/매출 that is aligned with your climate transition is estimated to change over time 및 설명 the assumptions underlying the estimation. Organizations that 선택 "Other, 직접 입력" in 열 1 "방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment" 해야 합니다 indicate whether you have obtained third party 검증/검증 귀사의 alignment information. Organizations disclosing alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 use this 열 to 설명 your 방법론 for calculating your taxonomy alignment 및 eligibility 및 how you have avoided double-counting. 만약 you have obtained third party 검증/검증 귀사의 alignment information, you will have the opportunity to indicate this in a subsequent 문항.

Our automobile manufacturing business currently produces both vehicles with internal combustion engines and electric vehicles. We have accounted as ‘aligned' the revenue generated from sales of electric vehicles, that have met the screening criteria for private passenger transport established under the CBI Taxonomy. We estimate that our revenue from EVs will increase in the future due to regulatory requirements and shifting consumer preferences. To estimate the percentage share in 2030 and 2035 we modelled the results from a recent consumer survey. To estimate the demand of EV vehicles in different jurisdictions we carried out a policy analysis and modelled the emergence of future regulations. In our calculation we excluded revenues from ICE vehicles and revenues from sales of equipment used in both ICE and EVs, as we classed such equipment as neutral.

Details of the methodology or framework used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition
Aquaculture constitutes a big part of our activities in Sweden. Given the lack of coverage of the agricultural sector under the EU Taxonomy, we have assessed the CAPEX associated with these activities using a bespoke methodology based on a combination of technical criteria established by the Aquaculture Act of Sweden and the principle of do no significant harm of the EU Taxonomy Regulation. As part of our net-zero by 2045 commitment, we intend to halve our footprint from fish feed and air freight. We are planning to increase the CAPEX associated with sustainable feedstock and special packaging to increase shelf life of our products from 5% to 10% of our total CAPEX by 2035.
We have assessed the alignment of our power generation activity from the onshore wind farm under the EU Taxonomy. The 8% figure disclosed represents the proportion of our total CAPEX associated with the substantial contribution of our onshore wind power generation activity to climate change mitigation and climate change adaptation in the reporting year. CAPEX associated with our onshore wind power plants, plant machinery and grid equipment are included. As part of our net-zero commitment to triple our renewable energy assets by 2045, we plan to increase our taxonomy-aligned CAPEX to 50% in 2030 and 60% in 2035. In calculating the figure as a total across both climate-related objectives, we followed the EU Commission's guidance to avoid double counting by separately counting CAPEX associated with activities contributing to climate mitigation and climate adaptation at the activity level.

Alongside our dairy business, we produce plant-based milks and yogurt. We have assessed the R&D expenses related to these plant-based products as aligned with our climate transition based on FAO's Sustainability Assessment of Food and Agricultural Systems (SAFA) guidelines. R&D expenses are accounted for in our financial statements as a subset of OPEX. Based on trends over the last ten years, we anticipate consumer demand for our plant-based products to continue to increase over time. Therefore, we estimate that the share of our total R&D that is on plant-based dairy alternatives will increase to 60% by 2035 to meet this demand.

Our company's operations include clinker and cement manufacturing activities which are eligible under the EU Taxonomy. In the reporting year, 15% of our total turnover was attributable to the manufacture of grey cement clinker and cement from alternative hydraulic binder - activities which met all criteria to contribute substantially to climate change mitigation under the EU Taxonomy by enabling the transition to 1.5C. Our operations in these activities are expected to expand by 2030 as we phase out our conventional cement manufacturing units. Therefore, we expect the proportion of our total turnover which substantially contributes to climate change mitigation under the taxonomy to increase to 35% by 2035. We also hope to report to an increased alignment of our activities in manufacturing of diisocyanates (currently not covered under the EU Taxonomy) with an expansion of technical screening criteria for activities in this sector under the EU Taxonomy.
guidance.textKo: Company A Response 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment Taxonomy under which information is being 보고 Objective under which alignment is being 보고 Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information 에 대한 선택 objective Financial metric Amount of 선택 financial metric that is aligned in the 보고연도 (currency) Percentage share of 선택 financial metric aligned in the 보고연도 (%) A sustainable finance taxonomy Other 직접 입력: CBI Taxonomy Climate change mitigation N/A 매출/Turnover 330,000 2 8 9 10 11 12 Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2030 (%) Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2035 (%) Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 Percentage share of financial metric that is taxonomy-non-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 Details 의 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition 4 30 N/A N/A Our automobile manufacturing business currently produces both vehicles with internal combustion engines 및 electric vehicles. We have accounted as ‘aligned' the 매출 generated from sales of electric vehicles, that have met the screening criteria for private passenger transport established under the CBI Taxonomy. We estimate that our 매출 from EVs will increase in the future due to regulatory requirements 및 shifting consumer preferences. To estimate the percentage share in 2030 및 2035 we modelled the results from a recent consumer survey. To estimate the demand of EV vehicles in different jurisdictions we carried out a 정책 analysis 및 modelled the emergence of future regulations. In our calculation we excluded 매출s from ICE vehicles 및 매출s from sales of equipment used in both ICE 및 EVs, as we classed such equipment as neutral. Company B Response 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment Taxonomy under which information is being 보고 Objective under which alignment is being 보고 Indicate whether you are also reporting eligibility information 에 대한 선택 objective Financial metric Amount of 선택 financial metric that is aligned in the 보고연도 (currency) Percentage share of 선택 financial metric aligned in the 보고연도 (%) Other 직접 입력: bespoke 방법론 using Aquaculture Act of Sweden 및 the principle of do no significant harm 의 EU Taxonomy Regulation N/A N/A N/A 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) 200,000 20 A sustainable finance taxonomy EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities Total across climate change mitigation 및 climate change adaptation 예 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) 480,000 35 8 9 10 11 Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2030 (%) Percentage share of 선택 financial metric planned to align in 2035 (%) Percentage share of financial metric that is Taxonomy-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 Percentage share of financial metric that is Taxonomy-non-획득 가능 in the 보고연도 30 40 N/A N/A 50 60 50 50 12 Details 의 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition Aquaculture constitutes a big part of our activities in Sweden. Given the lack of coverage 의 agricultural sector under the EU Taxonomy, we have assessed the 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) associated with these activities using a bespoke 방법론 based on a combination of technical criteria established by the Aquaculture Act of Sweden 및 the principle of do no significant harm 의 EU Taxonomy Regulation. 의 일부로 our net-zero by 2045 commitment, we intend to halve our footprint from fish feed 및 air freight. We are planning to increase the 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) associated with sustainable feedstock 및 special packaging to increase shelf life of our products from 5% to 10% of our total 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) by 2035. We have assessed the alignment of our 발전 activity from the onshore wind farm under the EU Taxonomy. The 8% figure 공개 represents the proportion of our total 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) associated with the substantial contribution of our onshore wind 발전 activity to climate change mitigation 및 climate change adaptation in the 보고연도. 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) associated with our onshore wind power plants, plant machinery 및 grid equipment are included. 의 일부로 our net-zero commitment to triple our 재생에너지 assets by 2045, we plan to increase our taxonomy-aligned 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) to 50% in 2030 및 60% in 2035. In calculating the figure as a total across both climate-related objectives, we followed the EU Commission's guidance to avoid double counting by separately counting 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) associated with activities contributing to climate mitigation 및 climate adaptation at the activity level. guidance.text:
Adaptation : in human systems, the process of adjustment to actual or expected climate and its effects, in order to moderate harm or exploit beneficial opportunities. In natural systems, the process of adjustment to actual climate and its effects; human intervention may facilitate adjustment to expected climate and its effects ( IPCC, 2018).
Capital expenditure (CAPEX): a measure of the value of purchases of fixed assets such as property, buildings, an industrial plant, technology, or equipment. Put differently, CapEx is any type of expense that a company capitalizes, or shows on its balance sheet as an investment, rather than on its income statement as an expenditure.  
Climate change mitigation: the process of holding the increase in the global average temperature to well below 2°C and pursuing efforts to limit it to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels, as laid down in the Paris Agreement.  
Emerging Climate Technology (ECT): a commercially promising technology that addresses climate mitigation challenges but needs to attract enough investment to deploy the technology and develop business models and markets for the product or services it produces. Eventually it may become a successful innovation deployed at scale, generating new markets or profoundly disrupting established (fossil-based) ones (Auerswald et al., 2005). For a more detailed definition and guidance, refer to the ECT initiative .   
Operational expenditure (OPEX): operating expenditure includes direct non-capitalized costs that relate to research and development, building renovation measures, short-term lease, maintenance and repair, and any other direct expenditures relating to the day-to-day servicing of assets of property, plant and equipment by the undertaking or third party to whom activities are outsourced that are necessary to ensure the continued and effective functioning of such assets.  
Revenue: gross income arising from the operations of an organization over a period of time.
Turnover: 'net turnover' means the amount derived from the sale of products and the provision of services after deducting sales rebates and value added tax and other taxes directly linked to turnover, as per Article 2(5) of Directive 2013/34/EU ( The Accounting Directive ).
guidance.textKo: Adaptation : in human systems, the 프로세스 of adjustment to actual 또는 expected climate 및 its effects, in order to moderate harm 또는 exploit beneficial opportunities. In natural systems, the 프로세스 of adjustment to actual climate 및 its effects; human intervention may facilitate adjustment to expected climate 및 its effects ( IPCC, 2018). 자본지출 (자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX))): a measure 의 value of purchases of fixed assets such as property, buildings, an industrial plant, technology, 또는 equipment. Put differently, 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) is 하나 이상 type of expense that a company capitalizes, 또는 shows on its balance sheet as an investment, rather than on its income statement as an expenditure. Climate change mitigation: the 프로세스 of holding the increase in the global average temperature to well below 2°C 및 pursuing efforts to limit it to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels, as laid down in the Paris Agreement. Emerging Climate Technology (ECT): a commercially promising technology that addresses climate mitigation challenges but needs to attract enough investment to deploy the technology 및 develop business models 및 markets 에 대한 product 또는 services it produces. Eventually it may become a successful innovation deployed at scale, generating new markets 또는 profoundly disrupting established (fossil-based) ones (Auerswald et al., 2005). For a more detailed definition 및 guidance, refer to the ECT 이니셔티브 . Operational expenditure (운영비용(운영비용(OPEX))): 운영비용 includes direct non-capitalized costs that relate to research 및 development, building renovation measures, short-term lease, maintenance 및 repair, 및 하나 이상 other direct expenditures relating to the day-to-day servicing of assets of property, plant 및 equipment by the undertaking 또는 third party to whom activities are outsourced that are necessary to ensure the continued 및 effective functioning of such assets. 매출: gross income arising from the operations of an organization over a period of time. Turnover: 'net turnover' means the amount derived from the sale of products 및 the provision of services after deducting sales rebates 및 value added tax 및 other taxes directly linked to turnover, as per Article 2(5) of Directive 2013/34/EU ( The Accounting Directive ).

sectorTagsKo: 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, Food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: This 문항 allows organizations to demonstrate the extent to which their spending 및 매출 is compatible with their climate transition, by assessing their alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy 및/또는 하나 이상 other 방법론. Such reporting is aimed at eliminating greenwashing in the 조직의 재무계획 in relation to its climate transition.
